title: krigia biflora subsp. glandulifera
intro: Krigia biflora subsp. glandulifera, also known as the glandular yellow-eyed grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, due to its compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The herbal preparations of Krigia biflora subsp. glandulifera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
